Motion To Dismiss For Forum Non Conveniens (Motions)
Off Calendar Apr-07-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. IGOR FRADKIN, Esq. of Downtown L.A. Law Group, LLP (Tel: 213-389-3765) for plaintiff;
Atty. STEFFANIE A. MALLA, Esq. of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP (Tel: 415-362-2580) for defendant Lyft, Inc.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT LYFT, INC.'s Motion To Dismiss For Forum Non Conveniens.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with a modification to grant defendant's motion as an amended motion to stay (not dismiss), as follows:

Lyft, Inc.'s "amended motion to stay for forum non conveniens" is granted. Plaintiff is an Ohio resident alleging injuries from a motor vehicle accident in Ohio involving another Ohio resident's vehicle. Witnesses, evidence and other sources of proof related to the accident are also in Ohio, and Lyft states willingness to be sued in Ohio. (See Stangvik v. Shiley Inc. (1991) 54 Cal.3d 744.)

Plaintiff's opposition relies on cases pending in our court where the "overriding issue" is "whether Lyft drivers are independent contractors or employees." (Id. at 2:22-27.) However, neither of those terms appears in plaintiff's complaint.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)
